marketState: neutral - conflicting signals, bearish storage/technical trend offset by above-normal cooling demand
summary: Front price sits almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.903 vs $2.917, -$0.014, FAIR), so there is no valuation edge today. Weather is the standout bullish input: demand is +8-15% above the 5y normal and rising in both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows, and the fair-value curve itself is modeled to climb toward $3.03 by +21D, with the front trading at a growing discount to that path. Working against that is a seasonality read that is bearish (-11.4pp vs median, only 31-50% odds of being higher 1-4 weeks out) but explicitly flagged Low reliability (50pp spread), a storage build that is +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a fresh +72 Bcf bearish surprise, a managed-money book that is net short (35th percentile), and a technical structure with a 50<200 EMA cross on every timeframe (though 4h/1d are oversold). Net effect: a modest bullish tilt over the multi-week horizon from weather/fair-value convergence, capped by the storage overhang; no clean edge intraday-to-days since price is fair and futures-curve data failed to load this run.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; consider half-size (0.5R) given low-reliability seasonality and the storage overhang
longTerm.rationale: Demand is running well above the 5y normal and still rising into peak cooling season, and the fair-value model itself projects a rise to ~$3.03 by +21D while front price already sits at a discount to that curve - a constructive setup for gradual convergence. A moderately crowded managed-money short (35th percentile) adds squeeze potential if heat persists.
longTerm.invalidation: Weather demand anomaly fades back toward the 5y normal, storage surprises keep printing bearish (further widening the +184 Bcf surplus), or price breaks and holds below the fair-value band's cheap side (~$2.55-2.60).

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is essentially at fair value (FAIR status, -$0.014 spread) so there is no near-term pricing edge, and technical trend is bearish (50<200 EMA cross) across 1h/4h/1d/1w with negative MACD on the longer intraday frames - even though 4h (RSI 25.3) and 1d (price below lower Bollinger band, RSI 34.2) are oversold. Futures-curve data failed to load this run, removing one of the four short-term inputs entirely.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a bullish reversal confirmation - daily RSI back above ~45 with MACD histogram turning positive and price reclaiming the $2.95-3.00 fair-value zone - to justify a tactical long, or (b) a clean breakdown and hold below ~$2.80 to justify a momentum short. Also want futures-curve data restored to confirm contango/backwardation before sizing a short-term position.

keyDrivers: Cooling demand +8-15% above 5y normal and still rising (1-7D and 8-16D both elevated)
Fair-value model projects a rise from $2.92 now to $3.03 by +21D, with front trading at a growing discount to that curve
Storage surplus +184 Bcf vs 5y plus a fresh +72 Bcf bearish weekly surprise caps upside
Managed-money net short at 35th percentile - crowded but not extreme, some squeeze risk
Technical structure bearish (50<200 EMA cross) on every timeframe despite 4h/1d oversold readings
risks: Seasonality signal reliability is explicitly 'Low' (50pp 10-90% spread) - wide uncertainty on the seasonal read used as top priority input
Futures curve data was unavailable this run (all proxy fetches failed) - cannot confirm contango/backwardation for short-term positioning
8-16D weather anomaly (+7.7% vs 5y) is milder than 1-7D (+14.6%), so demand support could fade quickly if the outlook shifts
